title: thymus zygiformis subsp. albanus
intro: Thymus zygiformis subsp. albanus, also known as the Albanian thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Albania, valued for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of T. zygiformis subsp. albanus include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and boost the immune system. This plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, essential oil, or tincture, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine and aromatherapy.
